items.title: How Vector Databases Search a Million Vectors Without Checking a Million
items.description:
Take the word "king." Your database does not store the word. It stores a vector: a list of 768 numbers that place king at a point in space, where words used in similar ways sit nearby.

That one move changes the whole problem. "Find something similar" becomes "find the nearest point." And f
